DefaultInstanceBrowseName.description: The default BrowseName for instances of the type
The default BrowseName for instances of the type

Undefined.description: The laser state cannot be indicated, for example because the device does not provide this information or because it is currently unavailable. This can be e.g. during the startup phase.

Ready.description: The laser is ready and laser programs can be started. No error state is active. In this state, laser emission is prohibited.

Active.description: In this state, safety clearances have to be set for processing and emission can be activated. For devices that can run programs themselves it indicates that a program is running on the laser device.

Error.description: An error state is reported from the laser device.

DefaultInstanceBrowseName.description: The default BrowseName for instances of the type

OperationCycleCounter.description: OperationCycleCounter is counting the times the component switches from not performing an activity to performing an activity. For example, each time a valve starts moving, is counted. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the component and shall not be reset when the component is restarted.

OperationDuration.description: OperationDuration is the duration the MachineryItem has been powered and performing an activity. This counter is intended for machines and components where a distinction is made between switched on and in operation. For example, a drive might be powered on but not operating. It is not intended for machines or components always performing an activity like sensors always measuring data. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the MachineryItem and shall not be reset when it is restarted. The OperationDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

PowerOnDuration.description: PowerOnDuration is the duration the MachineryItem has been powered. The main purpose is to determine the time in which degradation of the MachineryItem occurred. The details, when the time is counted, is implementation-specific. Companion specifications might define specific rules. Typically, when the MachineryItem has supply voltage and the main CPU is running, the time is counted. This may include any kind of sleep mode, but may not include pure Wake on LAN. This value shall only increase during the lifetime of the MachineryItem and shall not be reset when it is restarted. The PowerOnDuration is provided as Duration, i.e., in milliseconds or even fractions of a millisecond. However, the Server is not expected to update the value in such a high frequency, but maybe once a minute or once an hour, depending on the application.

DefaultInstanceBrowseName.description: The default BrowseName for instances of the type
The default BrowseName for instances of the type
The default BrowseName for instances of the type.

DeviceRevision.description: A string representation of the overall revision level of the component. Often, it is increased when either the SoftwareRevision and / or the HardwareRevision of the component is increased. As an example, it can be used in ERP systems together with the ProductCode.

AssetId.description: To be used by end users to store a unique identification in the context of their overall application. Servers shall support at least 40 Unicode characters for the clients writing this value, this means clients can expect to be able to write strings with a length of 40 Unicode characters into that field.

ComponentName.description: To be used by end users to store a human-readable localized text for the MachineryItem. The minimum number of locales supported for this property shall be two. Servers shall support at least 40 Unicode characters for the clients writing the text part of each locale, this means clients can expect to be able to write texts with a length of 40 Unicode characters into that field.

DefaultInstanceBrowseName.description: The default BrowseName for instances of the type.

DeviceClass.description: Indicates in which domain or for what purpose the MachineryItem is used.

HardwareRevision.description: A string representation of the revision level of the hardware of a MachineryItem. Hardware is physical equipment, as opposed to programs, procedures, rules and associated documentation. Many machines will not provide such information due to the modular and configurable nature of the machine.

InitialOperationDate.description: The date, when the MachineryItem was switched on the first time after it has left the manufacturer plant.

Manufacturer.description: A human-readable, localized name of the manufacturer of the MachineryItem.

ManufacturerUri.description: A globally unique identifier of the manufacturer of the MachineryItem.

Model.description: A human-readable, localized name of the model of the MachineryItem.

MonthOfConstruction.description: The month in which the manufacturing process of the MachineryItem has been completed. It shall be a number between 1 and 12, representing the month from January to December.

ProductCode.description: A machine-readable string of the model of the MachineryItem, that might include options like the hardware configuration of the model. This information might be provided by the ERP system of the vendor. For example, it can be used as order information.

ProductInstanceUri.description: A globally unique resource identifier provided by the manufacturer of the MachineryItem.

SerialNumber.description: A string containing a unique production number of the manufacturer of the MachineryItem. The global uniqueness of the serial number is only given in the context of the manufacturer, and potentially the model. The value shall not change during the life-cycle of the MachineryItem.

SoftwareRevision.description: A string representation of the revision level of a MachineryItem. In most cases, MachineryItems consist of several software components. In that case, information about the software components might be provided as additional information in the address space, including individual revision information. In that case, this property is either not provided or provides an overall software revision level. The value might change during the life-cycle of a MachineryItem.

YearOfConstruction.description: The year (Gregorian calendar) in which the manufacturing process of the MachineryItem has been completed. It shall be a four-digit number and never change during the life-cycle of a MachineryItem.

UIElement.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

data.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

StacklightOperationMode.description: Contains the values used to indicate how a stacklight (as a whole unit) is used.

Segmented.description: Stacklight is used as stack of individual lights

Levelmeter.description: Stacklight is used as level meter

Running_Light.description: The whole stack acts as a running light

Other.description: Stacklight is used in a way not defined in this version of the specification

StacklightMode.title: StacklightOperationMode
StacklightMode.description: Shows in what way (stack of individual lights, level meter, running light) the stacklight unit is used.

LevelDisplayMode.description: Contains the values used to indicate how a percentual value is displayed if the stacklight unit works in Levelmeter mode.

Other.description: Display fractions in a way not defined in this version of the specification.

DisplayMode.title: LevelDisplayMode
DisplayMode.description: Indicates in what way the percentual value is displayed with the stacklight.

LevelPercent.description: Shows the percentual value the stacklight is representing. The mandatory EURange Property of the Variable indicates the lowest and highest value and thereby allows to calculate the percentage represented by the value. The lowest value is interpreted as 0 percent, the highest is interpreted as 100 percent.

data.title: LevelDisplayMode
data.description: Indicates in what way the percentual value is displayed with the stacklight.

Shows the percentual value the stacklight is representing. The mandatory EURange Property of the Variable indicates the lowest and highest value and thereby allows to calculate the percentage represented by the value. The lowest value is interpreted as 0 percent, the highest is interpreted as 100 percent.

ChassisComponent.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of entPhysicalAlias object (defined in IETF RFC 2737) for a chassis component (i.e., an entPhysicalClass value of chassis(3))

InterfaceAlias.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of ifAlias object (defined in IETF RFC 2863) for an interface on the containing chassis.

PortComponent.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of entPhysicalAlias object (defined in IETF RFC 2737) for a port or backplane component (i.e., entPhysicalClass has a value of port(10), or backplane(4)), within the containing chassis.

MacAddress.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of a unicast source address (encoded in network byte order and IEEE 802.3 canonical bit order) of a port on the containing chassis as defined in IEEE Std 802-2014.

NetworkAddress.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on a network address associated with a particular chassis. The encoded address is actually composed of two fields. The first field is a single octet, representing the IANA AddressFamilyNumbers value for the specific address type, and the second field is the network address value.

InterfaceName.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on the value of ifName object (defined in IETF RFC 2863) for an interface on the containing chassis.

Local.description: Represents a chassis identifier based on a locally defined value.

Insert.description: The target is added. An error occurs if a name conflict occurs.

Replace.description: Existing records are updated. An error occurs if a name cannot be matched to an existing record.

InsertOrReplace.description: Existing records are updated. New records are created if no match to an exising record.

Delete.description: Existing records are deleted. An error occurs if a name cannot be matched to an existing record.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

PublisherId.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Unknown.description: Link is currently disconnected or initializing.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

OverrideValue.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

UserName.description: The rule specifies a UserName from a UserNameIdentityToken.

Thumbprint.description: The rule specifies the Thumbprint of a user or CA Certificate.

Role.description: The rule is a Role specified in an Access Token.

GroupId.description: The rule is a user group specified in the Access Token.

AuthenticatedUser.description: The rule specifies any non Anonymous UserIdentityToken.

Application.description: The rule specifies the combination of an application identity and an Anonymous UserIdentityToken.

X509Subject.description: The rule specifies the X509 subject name of a user or CA Certificate.

TrustedApplication.description: The rule specifies any trusted application that has been authenticated with a trusted ApplicationInstance Certificate.

Down.description: Not ready to pass packets and not in some test mode.
The interface does not pass any packets.

Testing.description: In some test mode. No operational packets can be passed.

Unknown.description: Status cannot be determined for some reason.

LowerLayerDown.description: Down due to state of lower-layer interface(s).

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Other.description: System has capabilities other than those listed below

WlanAccessPoint.description: System has WLAN access point capability

DocsisCableDevice.description: System has DOCSIS cable device capability (IETF RFC 4639)

TwoPortMacRelay.description: System has Two-port MAC Relay (TPMR) functionality.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

PortRef.description: Interface based on the port-ref MIB object.

SystemPortNumber.description: Interface based on the system port number.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

InProgress.description: The auto-negotiation protocol is running and negotiation is currently in-progress.

Complete.description: The auto-negotiation protocol has completed successfully.

Unknown.description: The auto-negotiation status is not currently known, this could be because it is still negotiating or the protocol cannot run (e.g., if no medium is present).

NoNegotiation.description: No auto-negotiation is executed. The auto-negotiation function is either not supported on this interface or has not been enabled.

SupportInitialPasswordChange.description: Indicates if the server supports the feature to require a password change after the creation of the user.

SupportDisableUser.description: Indicates if the server supports to disable a user.

SupportDisableDeleteForUser.description: Indicates if the server supports the configuration NoDelete for a user.

SupportNoChangeForUser.description: Indicates if the server supports the configuration NoChangeByUser for a user.

SupportDescriptionForUser.description: Indicates if the server supports to management of a description for the user.

RequiresUpperCaseCharacters.description: Indicates if a upper case ASCII character is required in a password.

RequiresLowerCaseCharacters.description: Indicates if a lower case ASCII character is required in a password.

RequiresDigitCharacters.description: Indicates if a digit ASCII character is required in a password.

RequiresSpecialCharacters.description: Indicates if a special character is required in a password.

InterfaceAlias.description: Represents a port identifier based on the ifAlias MIB object defined in IETF RFC 2863.

PortComponent.description: Represents a port identifier based on the value of entPhysicalAlias (defined in IETF RFC 2737) for a port component (i.e., entPhysicalClass value of port(10) or backplane(4)), within the containing chassis.

MacAddress.description: Represents a port identifier based on a unicast source address (encoded in network byte order and IEEE 802.3 canonical bit order) which has been detected by the agent and associated with a particular port (IEEE Std 802-2014).

NetworkAddress.description: Represents a port identifier based on a network address, detected by the agent and associated with a particular port.

InterfaceName.description: Represents a port identifier based on the ifName MIB object, defined in IETF RFC 2863.

AgentCircuitId.description: Represents a port identifier based on the agent-local identifier of the circuit (defined in IETF RFC 3046), detected by the agent and associated with a particular port.

Local.description: Represents a port identifier based on a value locally assigned.

SuppressCertificateExpired.description: Ignore errors related to the validity time of the Certificate.

SuppressHostNameInvalid.description: Ignore mismatches between the host name or ApplicationUri.

SuppressRevocationStatusUnknown.description: Ignore errors if the revocation list cannot be found for the issuer of the Certificate.

SuppressIssuerCertificateExpired.description: Ignore errors if an issuer has an expired Certificate.

SuppressIssuerRevocationStatusUnknown.description: Ignore errors if the revocation list cannot be found for any issuer of issuer Certificates.

StreamIdInUse.description: StreamID in use by another Talker

StreamDestinationAddressInUse.description: Stream destination address already in use

PriorityIsNotAnSrcClass.description: Requested priority is not an SR Class priority

MaxFrameSizeTooLarge.description: MaxFrameSize is too large for media

FirstValueChangedForStreamId.description: Changes in FirstValue for a registered StreamID

VlanBlockedOnEgress.description: VLAN is blocked on this egress port (Registration Forbidden)

VlanTaggingDisabledOnEgress.description: VLAN tagging is disabled on this egress port (untagged set)

FeatureNotPropagated.description: Enhanced feature cannot be propagated to original Port

BridgeDoesNotProvideNetworkId.description: Nearest Bridge cannot provide network identification for stream transformation

StreamIdTypeNotSupported.description: Stream identification type not supported for stream transformation

FeatureNotSupported.description: Enhanced feature cannot be supported without a CNC

PartialFailed.description: One or more Listeners ready, and one or more Listeners failed.

Disabled.description: The related TSN Stream is currently disabled.

Configuring.description: The related TSN Stream is in the process of receiving configuration parameters from the TSN Control Layer.

Ready.description: The related TSN Stream has successfully received and applied the configuration from the TSN Control Layer. The related TSN Stream is not fully operational as long as local preconditions (e.g. synchronization state) are not valid.

Operational.description: The related TSN Stream object is configured and all other required preconditions (e.g. synchronization state) for sending / receiving data are valid.

Error.description: The related TSN Stream object is in an error state.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

Body.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

NoChangeByUser.description: The user cannot change the password.

MustChangePassword.description: The user must change the password to get the assigned roles. If the password is not changed, the user has only the Role Anonymous.

Value.description: Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.
Stand-in for an unspecified data type.

FAILURE.description: Malfunction of the device or any of its peripherals.

OFF_SPEC.description: The device is currently working outside of its specified range or that internal diagnoses indicate deviations from measured or set values.

MAINTENANCE_REQUIRED.description: This element is working, but a maintenance operation is required.

Visual.description: Location indication through optical signals, such as blinking LEDs, if supported by the device.

Audible.description: Location indication through auditory signals, such as sounds or beeps, if supported by the device.

Firmware.description: The software update is used for the firmware of a physical device.

Application.description: The software update is used for an executable software.

Configuration.description: The software update is used for the configuration of a device or application.

Solution.description: The software update is used to install a solution with several software package to several components.

Current.description: The currently used version of the software identified by the CurrentVersion Object.

Pending.description: The pending version of the software that could be installed identified by the PendingVersion Object.

Fallback.description: The fallback version of the software identified by the FallbackVersion Object.

KeepsParameters.description: If KeepsParameters is not set, the device will lose its configuration during update. The Client should do a backup of the parameters before the update and restore them afterwards.

WillDisconnect.description: If WillDisconnect is set, the OPC UA Server will restart during installation. This can be the case if the update is about the firmware of the device that hosts the OPC UA Server.

RequiresPowerCycle.description: If RequiresPowerCycle is set, the devices require a manual power off / power on for installation.

WillReboot.description: If WillReboot is set, the device will reboot during the update, inclusive of embedded infrastructure elements like an integrated switch. An update Client should take this into account since the devices behind an integrated switch are not reachable for that time.

NeedsPreparation.description: If NeedsPreparation is not set, the Client can install the update without maintaining the PrepareForUpdateStateMachine. This can be used to support an installation without stopping the software.

LevelDisplayMode.description: Contains the values used to indicate how a percentual value is displayed if the stacklight unit works in Levelmeter mode.

Other.description: Display fractions in a way not defined in this version of the specification.

Red.description: Defines the intensity of the colour red

Green.description: Defines the intensity of the colour green

Blue.description: Defines the intensity of the colour blue

White.description: Defines the intensity of an additional white component. Shall be not provided when using RGB.

SignalColor.description: Holds the possible colour values for stacklight lamps.

Red.description: This value indicates a red lamp colour.

Green.description: This value indicates a green lamp colour.

Blue.description: This value indicates a blue lamp colour.

Yellow.description: This value indicates a yellow lamp colour (R+G).

Purple.description: This value indicates a purple lamp colour (R+B).

Cyan.description: This value indicates a cyan lamp colour (G+B).

White.description: This value indicates a white lamp colour (R+G+B).

SignalModeLight.description: Contains the values used to indicate in what way a lamp behaves when switched on.

Continuous.description: This value indicates a continuous light.

Blinking.description: This value indicates a blinking light (blinking in regular intervals with equally long on and off times).

Flashing.description: This value indicates a flashing light (blinking in intervals with longer off times than on times, per interval multiple on times are possible).

Other.description: The light is handled in a way not defined in this version of the specification.

StacklightOperationMode.description: Contains the values used to indicate how a stacklight (as a whole unit) is used.

Segmented.description: Stacklight is used as stack of individual lights

Levelmeter.description: Stacklight is used as level meter

Running_Light.description: The whole stack acts as a running light

Other.description: Stacklight is used in a way not defined in this version of the specification

Automatic.description: NC channel mode Automatic – execute CNC part programs.

MdaMdi.description: NC channel mode Mda/Mdi – manual data input and execution.

JogManual.description: NC channel mode Jog Manual – axis movement triggered by user.

JogIncrement.description: NC channel mode Jog Increment – incremental axis movement triggered by user.

TeachingHandle.description: NC channel mode Teaching Handle – teaching a machine tool by moving axes of the machine tool by hand.

Remote.description: NC channel mode Remote – the machine tool can receive CNC files via a remote access mechanism.

Reference.description: NC channel mode Reference – The machine tool returns to its reference point/ zero position.

Other.description: NC channel mode is different from the values defined in this enumeration.

Active.description: There is an active command being executed by the NC channel.

Interrupted.description: The NC execution is interrupted. Execution of a program in the channel can be restarted.

Reset.description: No NC command is active in the NC channel. E.g. channel is idle.

Undefined.description: The EDM spark generator state cannot be indicated.

Ready.description: Generator is initialized and can receive a set of technology parameters.

Active_Low_Voltage.description: Generator is switched on and is supplying pulses respecting the low voltage (≤ 25 V AC or ≤ 60 V DC) requirements of safety standard (ISO 28881).

Active_High_Voltage.description: Generator is switched on and is supplying pulse at high voltage (> 25 V AC or > 60 V DC).

Error.description: Generator is in an error state.

Undefined.description: The laser state cannot be indicated, for example because the device does not provide this information or because it is currently unavailable. This can be e.g. during the startup phase.

Ready.description: The laser is ready and laser programs can be started. No error state is active. In this state, laser emission is prohibited.

Active.description: In this state, safety clearances have to be set for processing and emission can be activated. For devices that can run programs themselves it indicates that a program is running on the laser device.

Error.description: An error state is reported from the laser device.

Manual.description: The machine tool is controlled manually, by the operator. Depending on technology specific norms, the maximum axis movement speeds of the machine tool are limited.

Automatic.description: Operating mode for the automatic, programmed and continuous operation of the machine. Manual loading and unloading workpieces are possible when the automatic program is stopped. Axis movement speeds are fully available to the machine tool’s ability.

Setup.description: Depending on technology specific norms, the maximum axis movement speeds of the machine tool are limited. In this mode, the operator can make settings for the subsequent work processes.

AutoWithManualIntervention.description: Operating mode with the possibility of manual interventions in the machining process as well as limited automatic operation started by the operator. Depending on technology specific norms, the maximum axis movement speeds of the machine tool are limited.

Service.description: Operating mode for service purposes. This mode shall not be used for manufacturing any parts. This mode shall only be used by authorized personnel.

Other.description: The machine operation mode is different from the values defined in this enumeration.

CapabilityUnavailable.description: The machine tool is not able to give a statement about the part quality.

Good.description: The part quality is determined good.

Bad.description: The part quality is determined bad.

NotYetMeasured.description: The PartQuality will still be determined in the machine tool to be either Good or Bad.

WillNotBeMeasured.description: The machine tool will not give a statement about the part quality.

CapabilityUnavailable.description: The machine tool is not able to give a statement about process irregularities.

Detected.description: A process irregularity has been detected.

NotDetected.description: There was no process irregularity detected.

NotYetDetermined.description: A statement about the process irregularity is to be expected.

Time.description: The tool life indicates the time the tool has been in use or can still be used. The value shall be given in hours (decimal value).

NumberOfParts.description: The tool life indicates the total number of parts that have been produced or can still be produced using the tool. The unit shall be “one”.

NumberOfUsages.description: The tool life indicates counting the process steps this tool has been used or can still be used (for example usages of a punching tool). The unit shall be “one”.

Feed_Distance.description: The tool life indicates the sum of the feed path covered by the tool and the workpiece relative to each other during machining. This value shall be given in one of the following units: millimetres, metres, kilometres.

Cutting_Distance.description: The tool life indicates the sum of the lengths that the cutting knife works in the workpiece. If the knife is not fixed, this includes the lengths of the arc segments of the knife path. This value shall be given in one of the following units: millimetres, metres, kilometres. This value is likely only available for serial production with clearly defined machining conditions.

Length.description: The tool life indicates the abraded length of the tool. This value shall be given in one of the following units: micrometres, millimetres, metres, kilometres.

Diameter.description: The tool life indicates the abraded diameter of the tool. This value shall be given in one of the following units: micrometres, millimetres, metres, kilometres.

Other.description: The tool life is indicated in a way not covered by the remaining enum values.

CapabilityUnavailable.description: The reason for locking the tool cannot be given.

ByOperator.description: The tool is locked by an operator.

ToolBreak.description: The tool is locked because a tool break has been detected.

ToolLife.description: The tool is locked because it reached a tool life limit.

MeasurementError.description: The tool is locked due to a measurement error of the tool.

Other.description: The tool is locked for another reason.

NumberBased.description: The tool is addressed using a single identifier.

GroupBased.description: The tool is addressed using an identifier for the group and a second one for the tool within the group.

Other.description: The tool is addressed by a different, custom defined system.

ISA95EquipmentDataType.description: Defines an equipment resource or a piece of equipment, a quantity, an optional description, and an optional collection of properties.

ID.description: An identification of an EquipmentClass or Equipment.

Description.description: Additional information and description about the resource.

EquipmentUse.description: Information about the expected use of the equipment, see the ISA 95 Part 2 standard for defined values.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the quantity

Properties.description: Any associated properties, or empty if there are no properties defined.

items.title: ISA95PropertyDataType
items.description: A subtype of OPC UA Structure that defines two linked data items: an ID, which is a unique identifier for a property within the scope of the associated resource, and the value, which is the data for the property.

ID.description: Unique identifier for a property within the scope of the associated resource

Value.description: Value for the property

Description.description: An optional description of the parameter.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the value

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

ISA95JobOrderAndStateDataType.description: Defines the information needed to schedule and execute a job.

JobOrder.title: ISA95JobOrderDataType
JobOrder.description: Defines the information needed to schedule and execute a job.

JobOrderID.description: An identification of the Job Order.

Description.description: Addition information about the Job Order The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages.

WorkMasterID.description: Work Master associated with the job order. If multiple work masters are defined, then the execution system can select the work master based on the availability of resources.

items.title: ISA95WorkMasterDataType
items.description: Defines a Work Master ID and the defined parameters for the Work Master.

ID.description: An identification of the Work Master.

Description.description: Additional information and description about the Work Master.

Parameters.description: Defined parameters for the Work Master.

items.title: ISA95ParameterDataType
items.description: A subtype of OPC UA Structure that defines three linked data items: the ID, which is a unique identifier for a property, the value, which is the data that is identified, and an optional description of the parameter.

ID.description: A unique identifier for a parameter

Value.description: Value of the parameter.

Description.description: An optional description of the parameter. The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages when writing. When accessing, the server shall only provide one entry in the array.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the value

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

StartTime.description: The proposed start time for the order, may be empty if not specified

EndTime.description: The proposed end time for the order, may be empty if not specified

Priority.description: The priority of the job order, may be empty of not specified. Higher numbers have higher priority.  This type allows the Job Order clients to pick their own ranges, and the Job Order server only has to pick the highest number.

JobOrderParameters.description: Key value pair with values, not associated with a resource that is provided as part of the job order, may be empty if not specified.

items.title: ISA95ParameterDataType
items.description: A subtype of OPC UA Structure that defines three linked data items: the ID, which is a unique identifier for a property, the value, which is the data that is identified, and an optional description of the parameter.

ID.description: A unique identifier for a parameter

Value.description: Value of the parameter.

Description.description: An optional description of the parameter. The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages when writing. When accessing, the server shall only provide one entry in the array.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the value

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

Description.description: Additional information and description about the resource.

PersonnelUse.description: Information about the expected use of the personnel, see the ISA 95 Part 2 standard for defined values.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the quantity

Properties.description: Any associated properties, or empty if there are no properties defined.

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

State.description: The State of the job order. The array shall provide at least one entry representing the top level state and potentially additional entries representing substates. The first entry shall be the top level entry, having the BrowsePath set to Null. The order of the subtstates is not defined.

items.title: ISA95StateDataType
items.description: Defines the information needed to schedule and execute a job.

BrowsePath.title: RelativePath
BrowsePath.description: The browse path of substates. Shall be null when the top-level state is represented.

StateText.description: The state represented as human readable text. Shall represent the same text as the CurrentState Variable of a StateMachine would.

StateNumber.description: The state represented as number. Shall represent the same number as the Number subvariable of the CurrentState Variable of a StateMachine would.

ISA95JobOrderDataType.description: Defines the information needed to schedule and execute a job.

JobOrderID.description: An identification of the Job Order.

Description.description: Addition information about the Job Order The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages.

WorkMasterID.description: Work Master associated with the job order. If multiple work masters are defined, then the execution system can select the work master based on the availability of resources.

items.title: ISA95WorkMasterDataType
items.description: Defines a Work Master ID and the defined parameters for the Work Master.

ID.description: An identification of the Work Master.

Description.description: Additional information and description about the Work Master.

Parameters.description: Defined parameters for the Work Master.

items.title: ISA95ParameterDataType
items.description: A subtype of OPC UA Structure that defines three linked data items: the ID, which is a unique identifier for a property, the value, which is the data that is identified, and an optional description of the parameter.

ID.description: A unique identifier for a parameter

Value.description: Value of the parameter.

Description.description: An optional description of the parameter. The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages when writing. When accessing, the server shall only provide one entry in the array.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the value

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

StartTime.description: The proposed start time for the order, may be empty if not specified

EndTime.description: The proposed end time for the order, may be empty if not specified

Priority.description: The priority of the job order, may be empty of not specified. Higher numbers have higher priority.  This type allows the Job Order clients to pick their own ranges, and the Job Order server only has to pick the highest number.

JobOrderParameters.description: Key value pair with values, not associated with a resource that is provided as part of the job order, may be empty if not specified.

ISA95JobResponseDataType.description: Defines the information needed to schedule and execute a job.

JobResponseID.description: An identification of the Job Response

Description.description: Additional information about the Job Response

JobOrderID.description: An identification of the job order associated with the job response.

StartTime.description: The actual start time for the order.

EndTime.description: The actual end time for the order.

JobState.description: The current state of the job. The array shall provide at least one entry representing the top level state and potentially additional entries representing substates. The first entry shall be the top level entry, having the BrowsePath set to Null. The order of the subtstates is not defined.

items.title: ISA95StateDataType
items.description: Defines the information needed to schedule and execute a job.

BrowsePath.title: RelativePath
BrowsePath.description: The browse path of substates. Shall be null when the top-level state is represented.

StateText.description: The state represented as human readable text. Shall represent the same text as the CurrentState Variable of a StateMachine would.

StateNumber.description: The state represented as number. Shall represent the same number as the Number subvariable of the CurrentState Variable of a StateMachine would.

JobResponseData.description: Key value pair with values, not associated with a resource that is provided as part of the job response, may be empty if not specified.

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

ItemNumber.description: ItemNumber defines an Identifier to identify the Type of the item (Material Identifier).

OutputInfo.title: OutputInfoType
OutputInfo.description: Bitmask indicating which of the optional fields are used for identification. If none is selected, only ItemNumber is used. Each selected optional field shall provide a value.

OrderNumber.description: OrderNumber defines an Identifier to identify the order. Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

LotNumber.description: LotNumber defines an Identifier to identify the production-group of the item (Lot Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

SerialNumber.description: SerialNumber defines an Identifier to identify the one entity of the item (Product Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

Quantity.description: Quantity defines the amount of material. This quantity can be specified in different ways, e.g. weight or number.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The engineering unit of the quantity.

ItemNumber.description: ItemNumber defines an Identifier to identify the Type of the item (Material Identifier).

OutputInfo.title: OutputInfoType
OutputInfo.description: Bitmask indicating which of the optional fields are used for identification. If none is selected, only ItemNumber is used. Each selected optional field shall provide a value.

OrderNumber.description: OrderNumber defines an Identifier to identify the order. Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

LotNumber.description: LotNumber defines an Identifier to identify the production-group of the item (Lot Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

SerialNumber.description: SerialNumber defines an Identifier to identify the one entity of the item (Product Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

ItemNumber.description: ItemNumber defines an Identifier to identify the Type of the item (Material Identifier).

OutputInfo.title: OutputInfoType
OutputInfo.description: Bitmask indicating which of the optional fields are used for identification. If none is selected, only ItemNumber is used. Each selected optional field shall provide a value.

OrderNumber.description: OrderNumber defines an Identifier to identify the order. Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

LotNumber.description: LotNumber defines an Identifier to identify the production-group of the item (Lot Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

SerialNumber.description: SerialNumber defines an Identifier to identify the one entity of the item (Product Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

Quantity.description: Quantity defines the amount of material. This quantity can be specified in different ways, e.g. weight or number.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The engineering unit of the quantity.

SimulationMode.description: Machine running in simulation mode with no workpiece involved.

TestMode.description: Machine running in test mode with a workpiece involved.

Unknown.description: Unknown state. Used when result is not known, e.g. because job order is still running.

Unsuccessful.description: Job order was not executed successful.

LotNumber.description: Identifier identifies the production group of the item (lot identifier).

SerialNumber.description: Identifier identifies the one entity of the item (product identifier).

ItemNumber.description: ItemNumber defines an Identifier to identify the Type of the item (Material Identifier).

OutputInfo.title: OutputInfoType
OutputInfo.description: Bitmask indicating which of the optional fields are used for identification. If none is selected, only ItemNumber is used. Each selected optional field shall provide a value.

OrderNumber.description: OrderNumber defines an Identifier to identify the order. Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

LotNumber.description: LotNumber defines an Identifier to identify the production-group of the item (Lot Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

SerialNumber.description: SerialNumber defines an Identifier to identify the one entity of the item (Product Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

ItemNumber.description: ItemNumber defines an Identifier to identify the Type of the item (Material Identifier).

OutputInfo.title: OutputInfoType
OutputInfo.description: Bitmask indicating which of the optional fields are used for identification. If none is selected, only ItemNumber is used. Each selected optional field shall provide a value.

OrderNumber.description: OrderNumber defines an Identifier to identify the order. Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

LotNumber.description: LotNumber defines an Identifier to identify the production-group of the item (Lot Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

SerialNumber.description: SerialNumber defines an Identifier to identify the one entity of the item (Product Identifier). Shall be provided if defined in OutputInfo.

StartTime.description: Output of first item from order.

EndTime.description: Output of last item from order.

Parameters.description: Parameters specific to the performance like pressure or temperature.

items.title: ISA95ParameterDataType
items.description: A subtype of OPC UA Structure that defines three linked data items: the ID, which is a unique identifier for a property, the value, which is the data that is identified, and an optional description of the parameter.

ID.description: A unique identifier for a parameter

Value.description: Value of the parameter.

Description.description: An optional description of the parameter. The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages when writing. When accessing, the server shall only provide one entry in the array.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the value

items.title: ISA95ParameterDataType
items.description: A subtype of OPC UA Structure that defines three linked data items: the ID, which is a unique identifier for a property, the value, which is the data that is identified, and an optional description of the parameter.

ID.description: A unique identifier for a parameter

Value.description: Value of the parameter.

Description.description: An optional description of the parameter. The array allows to provide descriptions in different languages when writing. When accessing, the server shall only provide one entry in the array.

EngineeringUnits.title: EUInformation
EngineeringUnits.description: The Unit Of Measure of the value

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.

CapabilityUnavailable.description: The machine is not able to give a statement about process irregularities.

Detected.description: A process irregularity has been detected.

NotDetected.description: There was no process irregularity detected.

NotYetDetermined.description: A statement about the process irregularity is to be expected.
